activities_projects.title: Comparative Economic Systems Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Evaluate the interdependence of the four factors of production in a shifting economy
steps: Hypothesize how a 50% increase in 'Fixed Capital' (machinery) impacts the 'Demand for Labour' in Palampur
Draft a thesis on the 'Law of Diminishing Returns' using the context of limited land (2 hectares) vs. increasing labor/fertilizer
Present a case for how 'Human Capital' acts as the primary catalyst for the other three factors
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Synthesizes complex economic theories with village-level data
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical consistency and theoretical depth (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Green Revolution: A Cost-Benefit Audit
activities_projects.objective: Conduct a multi-dimensional impact assessment of modern farming
steps: Quantify the 'Externalities' of HYV seeds: calculate the long-term cost of soil reclamation vs. short-term profit from high yields
Analyze the correlation between the 'Commercialization of Agriculture' and the depletion of the water table
Debate the ethics of 'Food Security' vs. 'Environmental Sustainability'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Critically evaluates the trade-offs of technological intervention in ecology
activities_projects.evaluation: Evidence-based argumentation and systemic thinking (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Capital Mobility and the Debt Trap
activities_projects.objective: Deconstruct the financial mechanisms that perpetuate rural poverty
steps: Compare the 'Compound Interest' effects of informal lending (money lenders) vs. formal banking for a 5-year period
Explain the concept of 'Collateral' and why it prevents Savita from accessing formal credit
Propose a 'Micro-finance' model that could provide working capital to landless laborers without traditional collateral
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands structural barriers in rural credit markets
activities_projects.evaluation: Financial accuracy and innovative problem-solving (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Labour Market Distortions: A Theoretical Model
activities_projects.objective: Investigate why market wages deviate from equilibrium and government mandates
steps: Graph the 'Supply and Demand' for labor in Palampur during peak vs. lean seasons
Explain the 'Sticky Wage' phenomenon—why wages remain low despite government-fixed minimums (₹300)
Assess the impact of 'Seasonal Unemployment' on the annual per capita income of a landless family
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies microeconomic models to real-world labor issues
activities_projects.evaluation: Precision in graphing and economic reasoning (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Land Fragmentation and Productivity Research
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the impact of inheritance laws on agricultural efficiency
steps: Simulate the division of Gobind’s 2.25-hectare land over three generations
Calculate the 'Economic Viability' of a plot smaller than 0.75 hectares
Research the concept of 'Consolidation of Holdings' as a solution to fragmentation
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Evaluates the link between land tenure systems and economic output
activities_projects.evaluation: Mathematical projection and policy recommendation quality (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Multiplier Effect of Infrastructure
activities_projects.objective: Trace the systemic impact of electricity and transport on village GDP
steps: Construct a 'Value Chain' showing how electricity leads to irrigation, which leads to surplus, which leads to trade, which leads to non-farm jobs
Estimate the 'Opportunity Cost' of a village that lacks an all-weather road to a town like Shahpur
Evaluate the role of 'Information Technology' (Kareem’s Center) in modernizing rural supply chains
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Conceptualizes the 'Multiplier Effect' in rural development
activities_projects.evaluation: Complexity of the connections drawn (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Entrepreneurial Risk and Working Capital
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the financial risks of diversifying into non-farm activities
steps: Draft a 12-month 'Cash Flow Projection' for a small manufacturing unit (e.g., sugarcane crushing)
Identify the 'Breakeven Point' where revenue covers both fixed and variable costs
Analyze why 'Risk Aversion' prevents small farmers from shifting to non-farm sectors
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies business accounting principles to rural enterprises
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of financial projections and risk assessment (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Global-Local Linkages: Palampur in the Global Economy
activities_projects.objective: Connect local production trends to global economic shifts
steps: Research how international prices of chemical fertilizers (petroleum-based) affect the 'Cost of Production' for a farmer in Palampur
Discuss the impact of 'Global Warming' on the cropping patterns of Rabi and Kharif seasons
Write a policy brief on 'Sustainable Intensification' as a future path for Indian villages
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Relates local microeconomics to global macro-trends
activities_projects.evaluation: Breadth of research and global-local synthesis (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Sociological Impact of Economic Change
activities_projects.objective: Assess how production methods change the social fabric of a village
steps: Investigate how the 'Green Revolution' increased the 'Wealth Gap' between the 450 families
Analyze the shift in 'Power Dynamics' when large farmers become money lenders
Discuss if 'Modern Farming' has led to social mobility or further marginalization of the landless
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Critically examines the social consequences of economic growth
activities_projects.evaluation: Sociological insight and depth of observation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Palampur 2050 Master Plan
activities_projects.objective: Synthesize all chapter concepts into a sustainable development proposal
steps: Create a strategic plan to double the 'Per Capita Income' of landless laborers without increasing land use
Incorporate 'Organic Farming', 'Agro-processing', and 'Digital Literacy' into the plan
Present a 'Sustainability Scorecard' to measure the success of the proposal over 20 years
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Demonstrates mastery over rural development planning
activities_projects.evaluation: Comprehensive nature and feasibility of the plan (10 marks)
